data.q: Which keyword is used to handle exceptions in PHP?
data.e: The catch block is used to handle exceptions thrown inside a try block.

data.q: Which keyword is used to throw an exception manually in PHP?
data.e: The throw keyword is used to create and throw exceptions in PHP.

data.q: Which block is always executed whether an exception occurs or not?
data.e: The finally block executes regardless of whether an exception was thrown.

data.q: What is the purpose of a try block in PHP?
o: To wrap code that may throw exceptions
data.e: Code that may generate exceptions is placed inside a try block.

data.q: Which PHP function is commonly used to display debugging information about variables?
data.e: var_dump() prints detailed information including type and value.

data.q: What is the difference between errors and exceptions in PHP?
o: Exceptions are manually throwable and catchable
data.e: Exceptions are designed for structured error handling using try/catch.

data.q: Which operator suppresses error messages in PHP?
data.e: The @ operator suppresses error reporting for an expression.

data.q: Which function is used to define a custom error handler in PHP?
data.e: set_error_handler() allows developers to define custom error handling behavior.

data.q: What happens if an exception is thrown but not caught?
o: The script may terminate with a fatal error
data.e: Uncaught exceptions usually terminate script execution with an error message.

data.q: Which predefined class is commonly used for generic exceptions in PHP?
data.e: The Exception class is the standard base class for exceptions in PHP.
